overview: Create the distributable fab/.kit/ engine directory containing all templates, skill prompts, and scripts needed to enable the Fab specification-driven development workflow in any project. Then bootstrap this sddr repo as a working Fab project with config, constitution, docs, and agent symlinks.
goals: Create a self-contained fab/.kit/ that works when copied to any project via cp -r
Implement all 10 skill prompt files as comprehensive, agent-agnostic markdown with full behavioral specs
Include a shared context preamble (_context.md) referenced by all skills for DRY context loading
Provide all 5 artifact templates matching the TEMPLATES.md spec exactly
Bootstrap this sddr repo as a working Fab project (config, constitution, docs, changes, symlinks)
Ensure flat-file symlinks in .claude/skills/ point into fab/.kit/skills/ for auto-updating
nonGoals: No CLI binary or system installation — pure prompt play only
No implementation of actual code changes — only the workflow engine files
Do not modify existing .claude/skills/ (commit, dev-browser, prd)
Do not create fab/current (no active change yet)
No package manager, no dependencies, no build step
successMetrics: All files in fab/.kit/ exist with correct content matching the spec
fab/.kit/scripts/status.sh is executable and outputs 'No active change' when run
fab/config.yaml, fab/constitution.md, fab/docs/index.md all exist with valid content
Copying fab/.kit/ to a fresh repo and running the init skill would bootstrap that repo

stack.framework: None — pure markdown files and one shell script
stack.hosting: N/A — files distributed via directory copy

rules: All skill files must be agent-agnostic markdown — no Claude Code-specific metadata, usable by any AI agent
Each skill file must include comprehensive step-by-step behavioral instructions (not references to external docs)
Skills must reference the shared _context.md preamble for context loading
Templates must match TEMPLATES.md spec exactly (including HTML comments as agent instructions)
Symlinks must use relative paths (../../fab/.kit/skills/) so they work after repo clone
status.sh must use relative path resolution from its own location
fab/changes/ created with .gitkeep to ensure git tracks the empty directory
VERSION file contains just the semver string: 0.1.0
qualityGates: test -f fab/.kit/VERSION && test -f fab/.kit/scripts/status.sh && echo 'core files exist'

for f in fab-init fab-new fab-continue fab-ff fab-clarify fab-apply fab-review fab-archive fab-switch fab-status; do test -L .claude/skills/$f.md && test -e .claude/skills/$f.md || echo "BROKEN: $f"; done && echo 'symlinks valid'
bash fab/.kit/scripts/status.sh | grep -q 'No active change' && echo 'status.sh works'
test -f fab/config.yaml && test -f fab/constitution.md && test -f fab/docs/index.md && test -d fab/changes && echo 'bootstrap complete'

stories.title: Create fab/.kit/ scaffold and VERSION
stories.description: As a developer, I want the base fab/.kit/ directory structure created so all subsequent stories have a home.
acceptanceCriteria: Create directories: fab/.kit/, fab/.kit/templates/, fab/.kit/skills/, fab/.kit/scripts/
Create fab/.kit/VERSION containing exactly '0.1.0' (no trailing newline beyond the standard)
Example: cat fab/.kit/VERSION outputs '0.1.0'
Negative case: VERSION must not contain anything other than the semver string (no 'v' prefix, no extra text)

stories.title: Create status.sh script
stories.description: As a developer, I want the quick-check shell script so I can see the active change from terminal.
acceptanceCriteria: Create fab/.kit/scripts/status.sh with exact content from ARCHITECTURE.md spec: uses relative path resolution from its own location, reads fab/current, parses .status.yaml for stage and branch
Script must be executable (chmod +x)
Script uses set -euo pipefail
When no fab/current exists, outputs 'No active change' and exits 0
Example: running bash fab/.kit/scripts/status.sh in a fresh project outputs 'No active change'
Negative case: when fab/current points to a missing change folder, outputs error message and exits 1
Script content must match the spec exactly: #!/usr/bin/env bash, set -euo pipefail, relative path resolution via $(dirname "$0")/../../current

stories.title: Create all 5 artifact templates
stories.description: As a developer, I want all artifact templates in fab/.kit/templates/ so skills can reference them when generating artifacts.
acceptanceCriteria: Create fab/.kit/templates/proposal.md matching the proposal.md template from TEMPLATES.md — includes {CHANGE_NAME}, {DATE}, Why, What Changes, Affected Docs (New/Modified/Removed), Impact, Open Questions sections with HTML comment instructions

Create fab/.kit/templates/spec.md matching the spec.md template from TEMPLATES.md — includes domain/topic sections, Requirement/Scenario format with GIVEN/WHEN/THEN, Deprecated Requirements section, RFC 2119 keywords note

Create fab/.kit/templates/plan.md matching the plan.md template from TEMPLATES.md — includes Summary, Goals/Non-Goals, Technical Context, Research, Decisions (with Why/Rejected), Risks/Trade-offs, File Changes (New/Modified/Deleted)
Create fab/.kit/templates/tasks.md matching the tasks.md template from TEMPLATES.md — includes Phase structure, task format with [P] markers, sequential IDs (T001...), Execution Order section

Create fab/.kit/templates/checklist.md matching the checklist.md template from TEMPLATES.md — includes CHK-* IDs, categories: Functional Completeness, Behavioral Correctness, Removal Verification, Scenario Coverage, Edge Cases, Security, Notes section
All templates must preserve HTML comment instructions (<!-- -->) as they serve as agent guidance
Example: each template contains placeholder variables in {CURLY_BRACES} for the agent to fill
Negative case: templates must not contain filled-in example data — they are scaffolds only (the examples from TEMPLATES.md go in the spec docs, not the template files)

stories.title: Create shared context preamble (_context.md)
stories.description: As a skill author, I want a shared context loading preamble so all skills can DRY-reference the same context loading instructions.
acceptanceCriteria: Create fab/.kit/skills/_context.md with the shared context loading convention from SKILLS.md
Must instruct the agent to always load: fab/config.yaml (project config, tech stack), fab/constitution.md (project principles and constraints)
Must instruct loading change context when operating on an active change: resolve active change via fab/current, load .status.yaml, load all completed artifacts in the change folder
Must instruct centralized doc lookup when writing/validating specs: read fab/docs/index.md, read specific docs referenced by proposal's Affected Docs section
Must instruct source code loading during implementation/review: read relevant source files from plan's File Changes or task descriptions, scope to files actually touched
Must include the Next Steps Convention: every skill MUST end output with a Next: line suggesting follow-up commands
Must include the Next Steps lookup table mapping each skill to its appropriate Next line
Example: a skill file would start with 'Read and follow the instructions in fab/.kit/skills/_context.md before proceeding'
Negative case: _context.md must not contain skill-specific behavior — only shared conventions

stories.title: Create fab-init.md skill
stories.description: As a developer, I want the /fab:init skill so I can bootstrap fab/ in any project and optionally hydrate docs from external sources.
acceptanceCriteria: Create fab/.kit/skills/fab-init.md as comprehensive agent-agnostic markdown
Must reference _context.md preamble at the top
Pre-flight check: verify fab/.kit/ exists, abort with guidance if not

Structural bootstrap (idempotent — skip if exists): create fab/config.yaml (prompt for name, description, tech stack), fab/constitution.md (generate from project context), fab/docs/index.md (empty index), fab/changes/ (empty dir), .claude/skills/ symlinks (create/repair), append fab/current to .gitignore

Source hydration (when [sources...] provided): support Notion URLs, Linear URLs, local files/directories; analyze content, map to domains, create/merge docs in fab/docs/{domain}/; update fab/docs/index.md
Must describe symlink creation: .claude/skills/fab-{name}.md → ../../fab/.kit/skills/fab-{name}.md for all 10 skills (excluding _context.md)
Must be idempotent — safe to run multiple times without damage
Output ends with: Next: /fab:new <description>
Example: first run creates all structure; re-run verifies and repairs
Negative case: if fab/.kit/ is missing, must abort with clear message, not create partial structure

stories.title: Create fab-new.md skill
stories.description: As a developer, I want the /fab:new skill so I can start a new change from a description.
acceptanceCriteria: Create fab/.kit/skills/fab-new.md as comprehensive agent-agnostic markdown
Must reference _context.md preamble at the top
Accepts <description> argument and optional --branch <name>
Generates folder name: {YYMMDD}-{XXXX}-{slug} where YYMMDD is today, XXXX is 4 random lowercase alphanumeric, slug is 2-4 words from description (all lowercase)
Creates fab/changes/{name}/ directory
Writes change name to fab/current (sets active change)
Git integration: if git.enabled in config and inside git repo — offer create branch, adopt current branch, or skip. If --branch provided, use that name directly. Record branch in .status.yaml
Initializes .status.yaml from template with stage: proposal, all progress pending except proposal: active
Generates proposal.md using fab/.kit/templates/proposal.md, loading constitution and config as context
Asks clarifying questions if intent is ambiguous (max 3 [BLOCKING])
Marks proposal complete once user is satisfied
Output ends with: Next: /fab:continue or /fab:ff (fast-forward all planning)
Example: /fab:new Add OAuth2 support → creates 260206-x7k2-add-oauth/
Negative case: if fab/config.yaml doesn't exist, prompt user to run /fab:init first

stories.title: Create fab-continue.md skill
stories.description: As a developer, I want the /fab:continue skill so I can advance to the next stage or reset to a previous stage.
acceptanceCriteria: Create fab/.kit/skills/fab-continue.md as comprehensive agent-agnostic markdown
Must reference _context.md preamble at the top
Normal flow (no argument): reads .status.yaml, identifies next artifact, loads template + context (including constitution), generates artifact, updates .status.yaml
Plan decision: when transitioning from specs to plan, evaluate if plan is warranted. If small/obvious, propose skipping to user. If agreed, record plan: skipped in .status.yaml
Auto-generates checklist (checklists/quality.md) when creating tasks stage

Reset flow (with stage argument): accepts specs, plan, or tasks. Cannot reset to proposal/apply/review/archive. Resets .status.yaml, regenerates target artifact in place, invalidates downstream (tasks reset to unchecked, checklist regenerated)
Context loading varies by stage as specified in SKILLS.md
Next steps vary by stage reached: specs done → 'Next: /fab:continue (plan) or /fab:ff or /fab:clarify', plan done → 'Next: /fab:continue (tasks) or /fab:clarify', tasks done → 'Next: /fab:apply'
Example: after proposal done, /fab:continue creates spec.md
Negative case: if no active change (fab/current missing), tell user to run /fab:new

stories.title: Create fab-ff.md skill
stories.description: As a developer, I want the /fab:ff skill so I can fast-forward through all remaining planning stages in one pass.
acceptanceCriteria: Create fab/.kit/skills/fab-ff.md as comprehensive agent-agnostic markdown
Must reference _context.md preamble at the top
Requires active change with completed proposal
Frontloads all questions: scans proposal for ambiguities across all planning stages, collects into single batch, asks once then proceeds without further interruption
Generates spec.md incorporating answers
Plan decision: autonomously decides whether plan is warranted (does NOT confirm with user, unlike /fab:continue). If skipped, records plan: skipped
Produces tasks.md referencing plan (if exists) or spec+proposal directly
Auto-generates checklists/quality.md
Updates .status.yaml to tasks: done
Flow: specs → plan (if warranted) → tasks (+ checklist)
Output ends with: Next: /fab:apply
Example: /fab:new Add spinner → /fab:ff produces spec + tasks in one pass
Negative case: if proposal is not complete, tell user to finish proposal first

stories.title: Create fab-clarify.md skill
stories.description: As a developer, I want the /fab:clarify skill so I can deepen the current artifact without advancing stages.
acceptanceCriteria: Create fab/.kit/skills/fab-clarify.md as comprehensive agent-agnostic markdown
Must reference _context.md preamble at the top
Guard: stage must be proposal, specs, plan, or tasks. If apply or later, suggest /fab:review instead
Loads current stage artifact + relevant context

Analyzes artifact for gaps: proposal (unresolved [BLOCKING], vague scope), specs ([NEEDS CLARIFICATION] markers, missing scenarios), plan (untested assumptions, missing research), tasks (missing tasks, wrong granularity)
Refines artifact in place — edits existing file, does not regenerate from scratch
Does NOT advance stage or update .status.yaml stage field — idempotent and non-advancing
Safe to call multiple times — refines further each iteration
Output ends with: Next: /fab:clarify (refine further) or /fab:continue or /fab:ff
Example: /fab:clarify on specs stage resolves 2 [NEEDS CLARIFICATION] markers
Negative case: if no active change, tell user to run /fab:new

stories.title: Create fab-apply.md skill
stories.description: As a developer, I want the /fab:apply skill so I can execute implementation tasks from tasks.md.
acceptanceCriteria: Create fab/.kit/skills/fab-apply.md as comprehensive agent-agnostic markdown
Must reference _context.md preamble at the top
Parses tasks.md for unchecked items (- [ ])
Executes tasks in dependency order, respects [P] parallel markers
After each task: run relevant tests, fix failures before moving on
Marks each task [x] immediately upon completion (not batched)
Updates .status.yaml progress after each task
Resumable: re-invoking picks up from first unchecked item
Context: config, constitution, tasks.md, spec.md, plan.md (if exists), relevant source code
Output ends with: Next: /fab:review
Example: /fab:apply with 12 tasks executes them sequentially, marking each done
Negative case: if tasks.md doesn't exist or tasks stage not reached, tell user to run /fab:continue or /fab:ff first

stories.title: Create fab-review.md skill
stories.description: As a developer, I want the /fab:review skill so I can validate implementation against specs and checklists.
acceptanceCriteria: Create fab/.kit/skills/fab-review.md as comprehensive agent-agnostic markdown
Must reference _context.md preamble at the top
Checks: all tasks [x] in tasks.md, all checklist items verified in checklists/quality.md, run affected tests, features match spec requirements, no doc drift
Re-reads each CHK-* item, inspects relevant code/tests, marks [x] or reports failure
On pass: output ends with Next: /fab:archive
On failure: presents options — fix code (/fab:apply with unchecked rework tasks), revise tasks (edit tasks.md then /fab:apply), revise plan (/fab:continue plan), revise specs (/fab:continue specs)
For rework: unchecks tasks with <!-- rework: reason --> comment
Updates .status.yaml: review: done on pass, review: failed on failure
Context: config, constitution, tasks.md, checklists/quality.md, spec.md, centralized docs, relevant source code
Example: review passes 10/12 checklist items, reports 2 failures with specific CHK IDs
Negative case: if apply stage not complete, tell user to run /fab:apply first

stories.title: Create fab-archive.md skill
stories.description: As a developer, I want the /fab:archive skill so I can complete a change and hydrate its learnings into centralized docs.
acceptanceCriteria: Create fab/.kit/skills/fab-archive.md as comprehensive agent-agnostic markdown
Must reference _context.md preamble at the top
Final validation: all tasks [x], all checklist items [x] (including N/A items)
Concurrent change check: scan fab/changes/ for other active changes referencing same docs, warn user

Hydrate into fab/docs/: read spec.md + plan.md + current centralized doc, rewrite doc to incorporate changes. From spec → integrate requirements. From plan → extract durable design decisions. Compare against existing doc to determine new/changed/removed

Hydration rules: new doc → create from template + add to domain index + add to top-level index. Existing doc → semantic update of Requirements section. Design decisions → add with change name for traceability. Add changelog row.
Update .status.yaml to archive: done
Move change folder to fab/changes/archive/ (no rename)
Delete fab/current (clear pointer)
Order: status update → folder move → pointer clear (fail-safe)
Output ends with: Next: /fab:new <description> (start next change)
Example: archives change, hydrates fab/docs/auth/authentication.md, moves to archive/
Negative case: if review not passed, tell user to run /fab:review first

stories.title: Create fab-switch.md and fab-status.md skills
stories.description: As a developer, I want the switch and status skills so I can manage multiple changes and see progress.
acceptanceCriteria: Create fab/.kit/skills/fab-switch.md: accepts <change-name> argument, matches against fab/changes/ (supports partial/slug match), handles ambiguous match (list and ask), handles no match (list available), writes full name to fab/current, displays switched change status

Create fab/.kit/skills/fab-status.md: shows current change state — change name, branch (if set), stage (N/7), progress table with symbols (checkmark=done, circle=active, empty=pending), checklist status, suggested next command
Both must reference _context.md preamble at the top
fab-status.md shows kit version from fab/.kit/VERSION
fab-switch.md: when no argument provided, lists all active changes and asks user to pick
Example: /fab:switch fix-checkout → 'fab/current now points to 260202-m3x1-fix-checkout-bug'
Example: /fab:status → displays formatted progress table with stage 3/7
Negative case for switch: partial match 'add' matches multiple changes → list all matches, ask user to pick
Negative case for status: no active change → 'No active change. Run /fab:new to start one.'

stories.title: Bootstrap this repo — config, constitution, docs, changes
stories.description: As a developer, I want this sddr repo bootstrapped as a working Fab project so it serves as a real-world reference.
acceptanceCriteria: Create fab/config.yaml for the sddr monorepo: project name 'sddr' (Specification-Driven Development Resources), description of the repo, context section describing the tech stack (markdown specs, shell scripts, Claude Code skills, Ralph agent), naming format {YYMMDD}-{XXXX}-{slug}, git enabled with no branch prefix, full stages config matching ARCHITECTURE.md, checklist extra_categories, rules for plan and specs

Create fab/constitution.md with Fab kit principles: Pure Prompt Play (no system dependencies), Docs Are Source of Truth, Idempotent Operations (all skills safe to re-run), Markdown-Only Artifacts (no binary formats), Portability (fab/.kit/ must work in any project via cp -r). Include governance section with version 1.0.0
Create fab/docs/index.md with empty initial index from TEMPLATES.md (table header with Domain/Description/Docs columns, no entries yet, HTML comment about /fab:archive populating it)
Create fab/changes/ directory with .gitkeep file to ensure git tracks it
Do NOT create fab/current (no active change)
Example: cat fab/config.yaml shows valid YAML with project.name = 'sddr'
Negative case: fab/current must not exist after bootstrap

stories.title: Create .claude/skills/ symlinks and update .gitignore
stories.description: As a developer, I want symlinks in .claude/skills/ pointing to fab/.kit/skills/ so agent integration auto-updates when .kit/ is updated.
acceptanceCriteria: Create 10 symlinks in .claude/skills/ with relative paths: fab-init.md → ../../fab/.kit/skills/fab-init.md, fab-new.md → ../../fab/.kit/skills/fab-new.md, fab-continue.md → ../../fab/.kit/skills/fab-continue.md, fab-ff.md → ../../fab/.kit/skills/fab-ff.md, fab-clarify.md → ../../fab/.kit/skills/fab-clarify.md, fab-apply.md → ../../fab/.kit/skills/fab-apply.md, fab-review.md → ../../fab/.kit/skills/fab-review.md, fab-archive.md → ../../fab/.kit/skills/fab-archive.md, fab-switch.md → ../../fab/.kit/skills/fab-switch.md, fab-status.md → ../../fab/.kit/skills/fab-status.md
Do NOT symlink _context.md (it's internal to .kit/, not a user-facing skill)
All symlinks must resolve (test -e returns true for each)
Existing .claude/skills/ contents (commit/, dev-browser/, prd/) must not be modified or removed
Append 'fab/current' to .gitignore if not already present
Example: ls -la .claude/skills/fab-new.md shows symlink → ../../fab/.kit/skills/fab-new.md
Negative case: running the symlink creation twice must not create broken or duplicate symlinks

stories.title: Validate complete structure
stories.description: As a developer, I want to validate the entire fab setup matches the spec so I can be confident it's correct.
acceptanceCriteria: Run all quality gates and verify they pass
Verify fab/.kit/VERSION contains '0.1.0'
Verify all 5 templates exist in fab/.kit/templates/
Verify all 10 skill files + _context.md exist in fab/.kit/skills/
Verify fab/.kit/scripts/status.sh is executable and outputs 'No active change'
Verify all 10 .claude/skills/fab-*.md symlinks exist and resolve
Verify fab/config.yaml is valid YAML
Verify fab/constitution.md exists with principles
Verify fab/docs/index.md exists with empty table
Verify fab/changes/ directory exists
Verify fab/current does NOT exist
Verify .gitignore contains fab/current
Verify existing .claude/skills/ (commit, dev-browser, prd) are untouched
Example: all quality gate commands exit 0
Negative case: if any check fails, report which specific file/symlink is missing or broken
